articles.title: Hong Kong Schools Ask Teachers to Learn More About Digital Education
articles.summary: Hong Kong is stepping up its effort to bring technology into classrooms. The government has introduced a plan requiring primary and secondary school teachers to complete at least 30 hours of digital education training every three years. This means teachers will spend about 10 hours per year learning how to use technology in lessons. Many schools already have tablets and smart screens in every classroom. Supporters say digital tools can help students learn at their own pace, giving stronger students harder challenges while helping others catch up. However, some educators worry that too much screen time in school, added to what children already use at home, may not always be the best approach for learning.

lv1: How many hours of digital education training must Hong Kong teachers complete every three years? (香港老師每三年須完成多少小時的數碼教育培訓？)
Name one digital tool mentioned that schools already have in their classrooms. (說出一種學校課室中已有的數碼工具。)
lv2: Why do you think the government wants teachers to receive training in digital education before using technology with students? (你認為政府為什麼希望老師先接受數碼教育培訓，才在課堂上使用科技？)
How might using tablets and smart screens in class help students who learn at different speeds? (在課堂上使用平板電腦和智能屏幕，如何幫助學習速度不同的學生？)
lv3: Some people believe that too much screen time in school is harmful, while others think technology improves learning. Which side do you agree with more, and why? (有人認為在學校過多使用屏幕有害，也有人認為科技能改善學習。你更同意哪一方？為什麼？)
Should all Hong Kong schools be required to give every student a personal tablet device? What are the benefits and risks of this idea? (香港所有學校是否應該規定每位學生擁有個人平板電腦？這個想法有什麼好處和風險？)

articles.title: Scientists Warn: Vaping Likely Causes Lung and Mouth Cancer
articles.summary: A major new scientific review published on 21 June 2026 has found that using nicotine vapes, also called e-cigarettes, is likely to cause lung cancer and mouth cancer. Researchers studied evidence from human health data, animal experiments, and laboratory tests. The results challenge the widespread belief that vaping is a safe or harmless replacement for smoking cigarettes. Scientists say the findings are serious enough to change how people think about e-cigarettes. Many young people around the world have started vaping, thinking it carries little risk. Health experts are now calling for greater public awareness and stronger rules around the sale of vaping products, especially to young people.

lv1: What two types of cancer did scientists link to vaping in this study? (科學家在這項研究中將電子煙與哪兩種癌症聯繫起來？)
What is another name for a vape or e-cigarette? (電子煙的另一個名稱是什麼？)
lv2: Why might young people mistakenly believe that vaping is safe, and what does this study tell us about that belief? (為什麼年輕人可能錯誤地認為電子煙是安全的？這項研究對這種想法有什麼啟示？)
What kinds of evidence did scientists use to reach their conclusions about vaping and cancer? (科學家用了哪些種類的證據來得出關於電子煙和癌症的結論？)
lv3: Should governments ban the sale of vaping products to people under 21 years old? What arguments support or oppose such a ban? (政府是否應禁止向21歲以下人士出售電子煙產品？支持或反對此禁令的論據有哪些？)

If vaping companies claim their products help adult smokers quit cigarettes, how should society weigh this benefit against the cancer risks identified in this study? (如果電子煙公司聲稱其產品有助成年吸煙者戒煙，社會應如何在這項利益與研究中發現的癌症風險之間取得平衡？)

articles.title: 神舟二十三號太空人在天宮空間站包糉慶端午
articles.summary: 中國神舟二十三號太空人朱楊柱、張志遠及黎家盈，上周在天宮空間站度過端午節，更特別在太空中吃糉子慶祝傳統節日。官方公佈的影片顯示，太空人按計劃進行多項太空科學實驗，並完成艙內應急醫療訓練，包括為隊友穿戴救護器具及測量艙內溫度。黎家盈升空約三周，身體已適應太空環境。此次任務展示中國航天員不但努力工作，更在太空中保持中華文化傳統，令不少市民感到驕傲。

lv1: 神舟二十三號上有多少位太空人？
太空人在太空站吃了什麼來慶祝端午節？
lv2: 為什麼太空人需要在太空站進行應急醫療訓練？
黎家盈在太空三周後，身體出現了什麼變化，你認為原因是什麼？
lv3: 在太空中保持文化傳統（例如慶祝端午節）對太空人有什麼心理上的意義？這對長途太空任務有多重要？
中國不斷發展太空探索計劃，你認為這對香港及亞洲青少年有什麼啟發？

articles.title: 香港擬將「高危照顧者」納入重點支援 防悲劇重演
articles.summary: 香港有議員提出，應將承受極大精神壓力的「高危照顧者」納入重點關注及支援系統。起因是近日有一名男子因長期照顧患病妻子而感到難以承受，最終釀成悲劇。事件令社會關注到本港龐大的照顧者群體，當中不少人長期獨力支撐，精神健康狀況令人憂慮。議員認為，政府應主動識別這類高危人士，提供適切的情緒輔導及社區支援服務，避免類似事件再次發生。社會福利界人士亦呼籲公眾多加關心身邊的照顧者。

lv1: 什麼是「照顧者」？他們主要負責什麼工作？
議員建議政府應為哪類人提供重點支援？
lv2: 為什麼長期照顧患病家人的人容易出現精神健康問題？
政府可以提供哪些具體支援，幫助「高危照顧者」減輕壓力？
lv3: 香港社會應如何在家庭照顧責任與政府社會服務之間取得平衡？個人、家庭和政府各應承擔什麼責任？
如果你是政策制定者，你會設計什麼措施來及早識別和幫助高危照顧者？請說明你的理由。
